seo.title: Google ADK Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Google ADK agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using Google ADK face heightened compliance risks due to multi-agent systems handling sensitive financial data, transaction processing, and regulatory decisions. Common pitfalls like circular agent dependencies and session state corruption can lead to unauthorized data access or inconsistent model outputs, violating SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC rules. Vex provides essential guardrails through before_agent_callback integration to ensure explainability, encryption enforcement, and audit trails.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over data processed by AI agents.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits agent inputs/outputs for data integrity and flags session state corruption before execution.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of transaction data at rest and in transit across multi-agent workflows.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces encryption checks and blocks unencrypted cardholder data in agent tool calls.

complianceRequirements.description: Demands data minimization and consent for processing personal financial information.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates PII redaction and consent verification in agent prompts before delegation.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires explainable AI decisions for credit scoring and suspicious activity monitoring.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures decision traces and enforces disclaimers in financial advice outputs.

industryRisks.name: Multi-agent circular dependencies
industryRisks.description: Agents endlessly delegate tasks, leaking sensitive transaction data across unauthorized paths.
industryRisks.example: Fraud detection agent loops with compliance agent, exposing unencrypted card numbers.

industryRisks.name: Gemini model switching inconsistency
industryRisks.description: Output format mismatches between models cause invalid credit decisions without disclaimers.
industryRisks.example: Model A approves loan without SEC-required risk disclosure; Model B rejects inconsistently.

industryRisks.name: Session state corruption
industryRisks.description: Long conversations corrupt customer session data, leading to AML false negatives.
industryRisks.example: Prioritizing wrong transaction flags customer as low-risk despite red flags.

industryRisks.name: Tool response format mismatches
industryRisks.description: Agents misparse encrypted payment tool responses, violating PCI DSS.
industryRisks.example: Payment gateway returns malformed JSON; agent processes plaintext card data.

implementation.title: Install and Setup Vex Callback for Google ADK
implementation.description: Add Vex as before_agent_callback to catch circular dependencies and enforce encryption.
implementation.code:
import vex
from google_adk import Agent, MultiAgentSystem

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex_client.guardrail(
    name="fintech-adk-guardrail",
    rules=[
        "block if circular dependency detected",
        "enforce TLS encryption on transaction tools",
        "require SOX audit trail"
    ]
)
def before_agent_callback(agent_call):
    # Vex validates before agent execution
    result = vex_client.validate(agent_call)
    if not result.valid:
        raise ValueError(f"Compliance violation: {result.issues}")
    return agent_call

# Apply to ADK multi-agent system
mas = MultiAgentSystem(agents=[fraud_agent, compliance_agent])
mas.set_before_callback(before_agent_callback)

implementation.title: Enforce Financial Disclaimers and Explainability
implementation.description: Guardrail ensures SEC-compliant disclaimers and traces credit decisions across Gemini models.

implementation.title: PCI DSS Encryption Enforcement
implementation.description: Blocks unencrypted transaction data in tool calls and validates TLS in multi-agent flows.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Google ADK in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized neobank deploys Google ADK for real-time fraud detection, using a multi-agent system where a triage agent delegates to specialized fraud, compliance, and payment agents powered by Gemini models. During peak trading hours, the system processes 10K+ transactions, with agents frequently switching between Gemini 1.5 Flash for speed and Pro for complex pattern recognition. However, circular dependencies emerge when the compliance agent repeatedly queries the fraud agent for AML verification, corrupting session state and exposing unencrypted transaction data in tool calls. Without guardrails, this leads to PCI violations and inconsistent credit decisions lacking SEC disclaimers.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex as before_agent_callback immediately blocks circular dependencies, enforces TLS encryption on all payment tools, and injects SOX-compliant audit trails. Model switching inconsistencies are resolved through standardized output validation, ensuring explainable credit decisions with mandatory disclaimers. The neobank avoided $2.3M in potential SEC fines, maintained 99.99% uptime, and passed their PCI DSS audit with zero findings.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and secure your Google ADK deployment in minutes.
